title: rubia peregrina subsp. requienii
intro: Rubia peregrina subsp. requienii, also known as the root of the French lavender or Requien's wild madder, is a subspecies of the Rubia peregrina plant that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The roots of this plant contain anthraquinones, which have been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubia peregrina subsp. requienii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Rubia peregrina subsp. requienii is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
